numeral.purpose: Grid digit; weight encodes authorship (700 user, 400 solver), tabular figures.

shadows.name: frame-lift
shadows.purpose: The board frame's only shadow; a faint ambient resting elevation, never over 8px blur.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Color-only transitions for selection, conflict, and solved states. Ease-out, 150ms.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, all transitions collapse to instant.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The Solve action. The one filled, brand-colored button on the page.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-fg); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.5rem; padding: 8px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 150ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--bg), 0 0 0 4px var(--primary); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: The Clear action. Outlined, recedes behind the primary.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: var(--bg); color: var(--ink); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.5rem; padding: 8px 20px; border: 1px solid var(--grid-line); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--surface); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--bg), 0 0 0 4px var(--primary); }

components.name: Sudoku Cell
components.description: The signature element. One square cell with default, selected, related, conflict, and solved states. Authorship shows in weight as well as color.
components.css: .ds-cellrow { display: inline-flex; border: 2px solid var(--grid-line-bold); border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; background: var(--bg); } .ds-cell { width: 3rem; height: 3rem; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: var(--bg); color: var(--ink); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 700; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; border: none; border-right: 1px solid var(--grid-line); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1); } .ds-cell:last-child { border-right: none; } .ds-cell--related { background: var(--cell-related); } .ds-cell--solved { color: var(--accent); font-weight: 400; } .ds-cell--conflict { background: var(--cell-conflict-bg); color: var(--danger); text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 2px; text-underline-offset: 2px; } .ds-cell--selected { background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-fg); }

components.name: Status Line
components.description: A single reserved line for the solve verdict. Holds its height when empty so the layout never jumps.
components.css: .ds-status { height: 1.5rem; margin: 0; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; } .ds-status--info { color: var(--muted); } .ds-status--error { color: var(--danger); } .ds-status--success { color: var(--accent); }

narrative.overview: A well-made instrument, not an app with personality to spend. A person arrives with a puzzle, types it in, and gets a correct verdict in seconds. The interface earns trust the way a good ruler or calculator does: exact, quiet, and disappearing into the task. One committed brand color (Layout Blue) carries selection, the primary action, and focus against deliberately neutral surfaces; warmth comes from the color and the type, never from a tinted background. Color never carries meaning alone: user and solver digits differ in weight as well as hue, and conflicts carry an underline as well as red.
keyCharacteristics: One committed brand color; neutral surfaces.
Authorship is unmistakable: user digits are bold ink, solver digits are normal-weight teal.
Redundant encoding: every color meaning has a second, non-chromatic cue.
Auto light/dark, WCAG AA, full keyboard operation.

rules.name: The Authorship Rule
rules.body: A user-entered digit and a solver-filled digit must never be told apart by color alone. User digits are Ink at weight 700; solver digits are Solver Teal at weight 400. Hue and weight always move together.

rules.name: The Neutral Surface Rule
rules.body: The body and cell background is pure white (or near-black in dark mode). Warmth and coolness live in the brand color and the type, never in a tinted surface. Cream, sand, and beige backgrounds are prohibited.

rules.name: The Auto-Theme Rule
rules.body: Every token ships a light and a dark value, switched by prefers-color-scheme. There is no manual theme toggle; the system follows the OS.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Grid digits always use font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. A 1 and a 9 occupy the same width so the grid never shifts as it fills.

rules.name: The Flat Frame Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat. The board carries one whisper-light ambient shadow; everything else is flat and reads through color, border, and weight. No element pairs a 1px border with a wide soft shadow.

dos: Do carry every color meaning with a second cue: weight for authorship, underline for conflict. Color alone is never the signal.
Do keep the body and cell background pure white in light mode and near-black in dark mode; let Layout Blue and the type carry the character.
Do keep grid digits on tabular-nums so columns stay aligned.
Do state verdicts plainly: "No solution exists", "Puzzle has multiple solutions", "Solved". No exclamation, no celebration.
Do keep Solver Teal at hue ~200 so it stays separable from Conflict Red for red-green color-blind users.
donts: Don't ship the toy/gamey register: no confetti, stars, streaks, or cartoon styling on a solve.
Don't drift toward the ad-heavy "free sudoku solver" look: cluttered, slow, untrustworthy chrome around the grid.
Don't use the generic AI-default surface: warm cream/beige backgrounds, a dusty accent, soft drop-shadowed cards, or an uppercase tracked eyebrow over a section.
Don't pair a 1px border with a wide soft shadow (the ghost-card tell), or round cards past 16px.
Don't distinguish solver digits from user digits by color only, and never signal a conflict with red text alone.
